The signature inside                   it is verified to confirm the struct is                   legitimate before we mark the hash revoked \u2014                   otherwise a malicious delegate could revoke a                   *forged* delegation hash by submitting a struct                   they invented."}}},"stateVariables":{"CAVEAT_TYPEHASH":{"details":"Caveat type hash for EIP-712 (only enforcer + terms; args excluded)"},"DELEGATION_TYPEHASH":{"details":"Delegation type hash for EIP-712"},"DOMAIN_SEPARATOR":{"details":"EIP-712 domain separator"},"OPEN_DELEGATION":{"details":"Open delegation sentinel \u2014 any address can redeem"},"ROOT_AUTHORITY":{"details":"Root authority constant \u2014 delegations with this authority are root-level"},"__gap":{"details":"Storage gap reserves 50 slots for future state. The contract      is currently NOT upgradeable (SC4 \u00a7 4.3.4 \u2014 DelegationManager      is singleton + re-deploy on bug), but the gap standardises      our storage discipline and keeps the option open."},"_revoked":{"details":"Revoked delegation hashes"}},"title":"DelegationManager","version":1},"userdoc":{"events":{"DelegationRedeemed(bytes32,address,address)":{"notice":"Emitted when a delegation is redeemed."},"DelegationRevoked(bytes32)":{"notice":"Emitted when a delegation is revoked."},"DelegationRevokedBy(bytes32,address)":{"notice":"Emitted when a revocation succeeds with the address that         submitted it (delegator or delegate)."}},"kind":"user","methods":{"hashDelegation((address,address,bytes32,(address,bytes,bytes)[],uint256,bytes))":{"notice":"Compute the EIP-712 hash of a delegation."},"isRevoked(bytes32)":{"notice":"Check if a delegation has been revoked."},"redeemDelegation((address,address,bytes32,(address,bytes,bytes)[],uint256,bytes)[],address,uint256,bytes)":{"notice":"Redeem a delegation chain to execute an action on behalf of the delegator.         The delegator's smart account executes the call via executeFromExecutor."},"revokeDelegation(bytes32)":{"notice":"Revoke a delegation by its hash."},"revokeDelegationByOwner((address,address,bytes32,(address,bytes,bytes)[],uint256,bytes))":{"notice":"Phase A.5 \u2014 authenticated revocation path that works for         Variant A (off-chain caveated delegation, never         registered) by deriving the hash from the delegation         struct + signature provided by the caller."}},"notice":"On-chain delegation management with caveat enforcement. Aligned with ERC-7710 patterns and MetaMask delegation-framework design: 1. Delegator signs a Delegation struct via EIP-712 2. Delegate calls redeemDelegation() with the signed delegation chain 3. DelegationManager validates signatures and enforces all caveats (beforeHook/afterHook) 4. Execution goes through the delegator's smart account via execute() Spec 007 Phase A.5:   - `redeemDelegation` is `nonReentrant` (SC5 \u00a7 6.2). Blocks nested     redemption via caveat-enforcer or target-call callbacks.   - `revokeDelegation(bytes32, bytes calldata)` is authenticated:     either the delegator OR the delegate may revoke, with the     verifier reading the delegation struct out of the explicit     signature payload so Variant A (off-chain) delegations can be     revoked too. C2 \u00a7 5 revocation-gap closure. Key ERC-7710 / DeleGator alignments: - Caveat args: redeemer-provided runtime arguments (excluded from delegation hash) - beforeHook/afterHook: enforcers revert on failure (no bool return) - Execute through delegator account, not direct target.call - Open delegations: delegate = address(0xa11) allows any redeemDelegation((address,address,bytes3.details: Phase A.5 (SC5 § 6.2) — `nonReentrant` blocks nested      redemption (a caveat enforcer or target call cannot      reenter this function).

revokeDelegation(bytes32).details: Legacy permissionless revocation by hash. Kept for backward-      compat with on-chain Variant B delegations (whose existence      is publicly registered, so revocation already requires the      submitter to have known the hash from a registration tx      they observed). New callers SHOULD use      `revokeDelegationByOwner` so the contract authenticates the      submitter against the delegation struct.

revokeDelegationByOwner((address,address.details: Authorization gate: `msg.sender` MUST be either      `delegation.delegator` OR `delegation.delegate` (the latter      blocks a random EOA from revoking a delegation it doesn't      hold, which would otherwise be a permissionless DoS vector      against the legacy `revokeDelegation(bytes32)` path).

params.delegation: The delegation to revoke. The signature inside                   it is verified to confirm the struct is                   legitimate before we mark the hash revoked —                   otherwise a malicious delegate could revoke a                   *forged* delegation hash by submitting a struct                   they invented.

hashDelegation((address,address,bytes32,.notice: Compute the EIP-712 hash of a delegation.

isRevoked(bytes32).notice: Check if a delegation has been revoked.

redeemDelegation((address,address,bytes3.notice: Redeem a delegation chain to execute an action on behalf of the delegator.         The delegator's smart account executes the call via executeFromExecutor.

revokeDelegation(bytes32).notice: Revoke a delegation by its hash.

revokeDelegationByOwner((address,address.notice: Phase A.5 — authenticated revocation path that works for         Variant A (off-chain caveated delegation, never         registered) by deriving the hash from the delegation         struct + signature provided by the caller.
